## Commitments from Vrijen (V)

| # | Commitment | Context | Urgency |
|---|-----------|---------|---------|
| 1 | **Introduce Chris to FOHI (Future of Higher Education) community** | V is the NYC co-lead of FOHI (~6,000 members globally). Offered Chris entry: "You can put in my name as a reference... mention a couple details about you, the size of your coaching and your audience, and specifically the fact that you're building 360, an edtech product aimed at K12." | Medium — Chris should apply soon while momentum is fresh |
| 2 | **Make intros within FOHI once Chris lands a linchpin school** | "Especially once you can lock down a corner like a linchpin school, you know, be happy to sort of make some intros within the community as well." | Conditional — triggered after Chris secures a paid pilot |

## Commitments from Chris

| # | Commitment | Context | Urgency |
|---|-----------|---------|---------|
| 1 | **Apply to FOHI Slack community** | Accepted V's offer. Needs to apply mentioning V as reference, his coaching audience (65K email list), and 360 product focus on K12. | Near-term |

## Action Items

### For Chris (recommended by V, not formally committed)

| # | Action | Rationale | Quote |
|---|--------|-----------|-------|
| 1 | **Find a paid design partner pilot** — target a school district willing to put money down and work with the product as-is, with limited feature requests | Avoid death-by-pilot cycle. Revenue + validation > free pilots that demand custom features. | "My ideal would be trying to find a pilot where they put some money down. Doesn't have to be a lot, but it has to be some. And they are allowed to ask for some changes, but not a lot." |
| 2 | **Run a focused push of customer interviews** to sharpen ICP | V's core lesson: "Every time I thought we were being too narrow, we were still being too broad." Chris needs to drill down from "K12 administrators" to a specific sub-segment. | "Really maybe like a push of customer interviews to find out, okay, all of the people that I think are my ICP, who are my niche." |
| 3 | **Consider hiring an SDR** for outbound lead generation while Chris continues closing | Founder should stay on closing calls for customer research, messaging refinement, and network building. Delegate high-volume outreach. | "If you're going to optimize for one, get someone talented, hard working to do your fucking dialing and you still close." |
| 4 | **Consider building in public** on social media as a brand-building supplement | The executive function coaching space is emotive — authenticity resonates. Combined with 65K email list, social content could amplify reach. | "Building in public is especially for your use case, a really good one because it's a very emotive use case." |
| 5 | **Explore creating a user community group** (Facebook group or similar) for engaged users and trial users | Chris's own idea, validated by V. Sits between "user research group" and full community. Start small with top/most committed users. | Chris: "What if I started just like a simple Facebook group of, you know, users?" |

### For V

| # | Action | Rationale |
|---|--------|-----------|
| 1 | **Follow up on FOHI intro** — confirm Chris applied, provide any needed support for application | V offered the connection; should close the loop |
| 2 | **Review 360 product/website** | V asked Chris to paste the link in chat; should review for deeper advisory if relationship continues |

## Open Questions (Unresolved)

- **How narrow should Chris's ICP go?** V flagged this as critical but the conversation didn't land on a specific sub-segment beyond "K12 administrators."
- **Pricing for organizational pilots** — no specific numbers discussed beyond the current $10/mo individual, $20/mo supporter tiers.
- **Relationship structure going forward** — unclear whether this becomes ongoing advisory, a single helpful conversation, or something more formal.
